threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'River Lea' instead of 'Lea' like the locals do. That and you’re not layering yet for the unpredictable weather!
Saw a guy on the 133 last night singing opera. It was oddly beautiful, but everyone pretended not to notice. Only in Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a café with a cat lounging on the bar counter, and no one batted an eye. That’s when I knew I truly belonged.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how in Neukölln, old pubs turn into art galleries overnight? One day it's a beer hall, next it's an exhibition space. Just like that.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can name the taco truck in front of the 24th St. BART and have a favorite salsa from there.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver singing along to the radio yesterday. Didn't miss a beat while navigating a packed 14-Mission. Classic SF vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all the plaça are for lounging. But on weekends, locals sweep in for a quick café, then rush off to the next barra.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still staring at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, while locals zip past them, knowing the shortcuts.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to complain about the 'charm' of the old houses while hearing sirens every night. That’s a rookie mistake.
Last night on the 3 bus, a guy serenaded the whole ride with karaoke. Best show I've seen in ages, even the driver grinned!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a family using a rollator and adapting their route around broken pavement while sharing jokes. Community in motion.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you have a pop-up vegan café in an old laundromat, where laundry machines double as seating. That's quintessential Berlin spirit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here if they know which taco truck has the best salsa—and it ain’t the one everyone posts on Instagram.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the N-Judah this morning. Totally nails the quirky spirit of Muni—only in SF can you go from chaos to a solo concert in seconds.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how quiet Gràcia gets after 10 PM. The bustling plazas empty out, revealing a different, peaceful side of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them trying to navigate the winding streets while holding a paper map, oblivious to how everyone else uses Google Maps.
